evidence_quotes.quote: sunsetting online-only games such as "The Crew" that have been fully paid for yet discontinued to exist by their company ubisoft.
as a consumer we paid for the games and as a right, we get to keep them.

paragraphs.text: Hello European Commision, I fully support your initiative but I'am also concerned over the how game companies have lately been sunsetting online-only games such as "The Crew" that have been fully paid for yet discontinued to exist by their company ubisoft. I fear that more games will follow suit and I wish for your initiative to include that games should be protected and perserved, online or not because as a consumer we paid for the games and as a right, we get to keep them. I would also like to suggest to put something to prevent payment procressers to not censor and prevent a consumer from being able to buy what he wants as long as it is legal, seeing as visa and mastercard have a duoploy. Please, consider these suggestions as game companies and payment processers have lately been overreaching with their power and destroying any sort of possibility to preserve games.
